title: End point instant heating/cooling system and method of providing instant heated/cooled fluid to an end point
abstract: An end point instant heating system (10) comprising a pipe (16) leading to the end point (20). A heating element (24) is located adjacent the end point (20) operable to heat fluid (1) contained in the pipe (16). At least one temperature probe (26a, 26b) is located proximate the heating element (24). A control system (28) operates to receive first temperature signals from a first temperature probe (26a). If the first temperature signals so received are less than a preset temperature level, the control system (208) operates the heating element (24) to apply greater heat to fluid (1) contained in the pipe (16).
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1 . An end point instant heating system comprising: a pipe leading to the end point; a heating element located adjacent the end point operable to heat fluid contained in the pipe; at least one temperature probe located proximate the heating element; and a control system; where the control system operates to receive first temperature signals from a first temperature probe and, if the first temperature signals so received are less than a preset temperature level, operate to heating element to apply greater heat to fluid contained in the pipe. 2. An end point instant heating system according to claim 1 further comprising a central heating system that provides fluid to the pipe, where the control system operates to receive second temperature signals from a second temperature probe located upstream of the heating element in the direction of fluid flow and, if the second temperature signals so received are equal to or more than the preset temperature level, operates the heating element to apply a reduced heat, or no heat, to fluid contained in the pipe. 3. An end point instant heating system according to any preceding claim, where the heating element takes the form of an induction coil wound about the pipe. 4. An end point instant heating system according to any preceding claim, further comprising mixing means located at or downstream of the heating element, the mixing means operable to create turbulence in the fluid flowing through the pipe. 5. An end point instant heating system according to claim 4, where the mixing means takes the form of static mixing veins. 6. An end point instant heating system according to claim 5, where the static mixing veins are arranged to form a lattice. 7. An end point instant heating system according to any preceding claim, where the power source provides power to the heating element through wireless means. 8. An end point instant heating system according to any preceding claim, where the heating element is connected to a rechargeable battery, such that power not used by the heating element trickle charges the rechargeable battery. 9. An end point instant heating system according to any preceding claim, where the end point instant heating system is incorporated into a mixer tap. 10. An end point instant heating system according to any preceding claim, where the pipe is coated with a lagging. 1 1 . An end point instant cooling system comprising: a pipe leading to the end point; a cooling element located adjacent the end point operable to cool fluid contained in the pipe; at least one temperature probe located proximate the cooling element; and a control system, where the control system operates to receive first temperature signals from a first temperature probe and, if the first temperature signals so received are greater than a preset temperature level, operate the cooling element to provide a greater cooling force to fluid contained in the pipe. 12. An end point instant cooling system according to claim 1 1 , further comprising a central fluid reservoir that provides fluid to the pipe, where the control system operates to receive second temperature signals from a second temperature probe located upstream of the cooling element in the direction of fluid flow and, if the second temperature signals so received are equal to or lower than the preset temperature level, operates the cooling element to apply a reduced cooling force, or no cooling force, to fluid contained in the pipe. 13. An end point instant cooling system according to claim 1 1 or claim 12, further comprising mixing means located at or downstream of the cooling element, the mixing means operable to create turbulence in the fluid flowing through the pipe. 14. An end point instant cooling system according to claim 13, where the mixing means takes the form of static mixing veins. 15. An end point instant cooling system according to claim 14, where the static mixing veins are arranged to form a lattice. 16. An end point instant cooling system according to any one of claims 1 1 to 15, where the power source provides power to the cooling element through wireless means. 17. An end point instant cooling system according to any one of claims 1 1 to 16, where the cooling element is connected to a rechargeable battery, such that power not used by the cooling element trickle charges the rechargeable battery. 18. An end point instant cooling system according to any one of claims 1 1 to 17, where the end point instant cooling system is incorporated into a mixer tap. 19. An end point instant cooling system according to any one of claims 1 1 to 18, where the pipe is coated with a lagging. 20. A method of providing instant heated fluid to an end point, the method comprising the steps of: providing fluid from a first point to the end point by way of a pipe; receiving first temperature signals from a first temperature probe located proximate a heating element; if the first temperature signals so received are less than a preset temperature level, operate to heat fluid in the pipe by way of a heating element, the heating element located adjacent the end point. 21 . A method of providing instant heated fluid to an end point according to claim 20, the method further comprising the steps of: receiving second temperature signals from a second temperature probe located upstream of the heating element in the direction of fluid flow; and operating the heating element to apply a reduced heat, or no heat, to fluid contained in the pipe if the received second temperature signals are equal to or more than the preset temperature level. 22. A method of providing instant heated fluid to an end point according to claim 20 or claim 21 , the method further including the step of creating turbulence in the fluid flowing through the pipe. 23. A method of providing instant heated fluid to an end point according to any one of claims 20 to 22, further comprising the step of wirelessly supplying power to the heating element. 24. A method of providing instant heated fluid to an end point according to any one of claims 20 to 23, further comprising the step of using power not otherwise required by the heating element to trickle charge a rechargeable battery. 25. A method of providing instant cooled fluid to an end point, the method comprising the steps of: providing fluid from a first point to the end point by way of a pipe; receiving first temperature signals from a first temperature probe located proximate a cooling element; 

 if the first temperature signals so received are higher than a preset temperature level, operate to cool fluid in the pipe by way of a cooling element, the cooling element located adjacent the end point. 26. A method of providing instant cooled fluid to an end point according to claim 25, the method further comprising the steps of: receiving second temperature signals from a second temperature probe located upstream of the cooling element in the direction of fluid flow; and operating the cooling element to apply a reduced cooling force, or no cooling force, to fluid contained in the pipe if the received second temperature signals are equal to or lower than the preset temperature level. 27. A method of providing instant cooled fluid to an end point according to claim 25 or claim 26, the method further including the step of creating turbulence in the fluid flowing through the pipe. 28. A method of providing instant cooled fluid to an end point according to any one of claims 25 to 27, further comprising the step of wirelessly supplying power to the cooling element. 29. A method of providing instant cooled fluid to an end point according to any one of claims 25 to 28, further comprising the step of using power not otherwise required by the cooling element to trickle charge a rechargeable battery.
